title: $name - FEDERAL RESERVE BANK of NEW YORK

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: This is the New York Fed's news events listing page, containing multiple unrelated press releases spanning 2024-2026. The page aggregates announcements on FX intervention reports, household debt and credit data, inflation and labor market expectations surveys, board appointments, and research on tokenization/smart contracts. Most recent item is dated March 26, 2026.
key_points: SOURCE_TYPE is listing_page (homepage) - non-archiveable per rules
Multiple unrelated press releases are aggregated on this page
Recent FX-related items: Fed did not intervene in Q4 2025; Treasury did intervene during same period (Feb 12, 2026)
Jan 2025 item announced Michelle Neal's resignation as Head of Markets Group; Apr 2025 item announced Anna Nordstrom as replacement
May 2024 item mentioned joint study on feasibility of central bank operations using tokenization and smart contracts
Regular recurring releases: quarterly FX intervention reports, monthly SCE (Survey of Consumer Expectations) on inflation and labor market
Contains household debt balance reports and community development financial institution reports
why_it_matters: This listing page does not contain a specific archiveable document. Individual press releases from this page would need to be extracted and processed separately for meaningful research records.
market_structure_context: Potential market structure relevance in: (1) FX intervention transparency reports (Q4 2025: Fed inactive, Treasury active), (2) Tokenization/smart contracts research (May 2024 joint study), (3) Markets Group leadership transitions. However, these are not directly accessible from this listing page.
notes: NON-ARCHIVABLE: This is a homepage/listing page containing an index of press releases. Per CORE RULE 6, listing pages are non-archiveable and should be stated as such. The individual press releases linked from this page (e.g., specific FX intervention reports, SCE data releases, appointment announcements) should be crawled and processed individually for proper archival. The most recent substantive items date to March 2026 (5 days ago from TODAY).
